source: All-In Podcast — 2025 predictions (bestie guest Gavin Baker)
summary: Government service providers / federal contractors are the biggest business losers of 2025 due to DOGE scrutiny
summary_extended: Baker names government service providers and federal contractors as his biggest business losers of 2025, arguing that any company deriving more than 35% of its total revenue from the US government is at severe risk of disruption from DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) scrutiny. His core reasoning is structural: Cost Plus contracts, which dominate federal contracting, are incentivized toward inefficiency and bloat, making them prime targets for aggressive cost-cutting and renegotiation. Baker specifically calls out traditional defense primes and IT consulting firms that are heavily dependent on government work, naming Accenture, Cognizant, Tata, HCL, and Infosys as facing existential pressure. The investment implication is to avoid or short any company where US government revenue exceeds the 35% threshold, as DOGE-driven contract scrutiny could materially impair revenues and margins in the near term.
detail: Baker explicitly states that any company with US government revenue exceeding 35% of total revenue is at severe risk. DOGE will scrutinize Cost Plus contracts, which are structurally incentivized toward inefficiency. Traditional defense primes and consulting firms (Accenture, Cognizant, Tata, HCL, Infosys) face existential pressure.
confidence_evidence: Baker names this as his explicit biggest business loser prediction with a specific revenue threshold (>35% government)
quote_fragment: you do not want to have the United States government at any level is over 35% of your revenue

[RE-AUDIT: Baker did not specifically name Lockheed Martin in connection with this thesis. He named Accenture, Cognizant, Tata, HCL, and Infosys explicitly. LMT was inferred as a defense prime fitting the >35% threshold, but Baker did not specifically connect it to this thesis.]

RTX (Raytheon Technologies) is a major defense prime with heavy US government revenue concentration, directly fitting Baker's >35% government revenue risk profile. Boeing's defense and government services segment generates substantial US government revenue, placing it squarely within Baker's stated risk threshold for DOGE scrutiny. Cognizant is explicitly named by Baker as a firm at risk, with meaningful US government IT services revenue.
Infosys is explicitly named by Baker as facing pressure from DOGE scrutiny on federal IT consulting contracts.
HCL Technologies is not US-listed (trades on NSE/BSE in India); it should not be included as a US-listed equity ticker in this audit. Baker named it but it is not actionable as a US-listed equity.

Science Applications International Corporation is a pure-play US government IT services contractor with nearly 100% of revenue from US government clients, making it an unambiguous direct subject of Baker's >35% government revenue thesis.
